title: Smaller type-design fixes: progress.Sink rename, lsp shutdown, llm.Role + Jitter bound, store chunk dedup + lenses JSON, Outcome invariants
description: Grab-bag of low-risk items: rename progress.Sink -> EventSink (name collides with report.Sink); fix lsp Manager shutdown coordination (two independent flags m.closed + ms.failed leave a race window, manager.go ~25/44); give llm a type Role (currently a bare string alias) and bound RetryConfig.Jitter to [0,1]; migrate store CorroboratingLenses from the hand-rolled comma-encoding to JSON (consistent with sibling columns) and extract the chunked-query helper duplicated in store filestate.go/cartographer.go; document/enforce agent.Outcome invariants (Truncated implies TruncationReason set).
acceptance_criteria: progress sink renamed with callers updated; lsp shutdown uses a single coordinated signal; llm.Role typed and Jitter validated; store chunk helper deduped and lenses stored as JSON; agent.Outcome invariant documented/enforced; build+test green.

title: codenav typing: OutlineEntry.Kind enum, analyzer rule->lens tables, tagger struct key, exhaustive queryKind, miner class validation
description: treesitter/analyzer/miner type fixes: OutlineEntry.Kind stringly-typed -> type Kind enum (outline.go ~13); analyzer rule->lens mapping is 3 separate switch funcs over magic lens strings -> data tables + a LensName type (analyzer.go ~70/154/235/318); tagger cache key is name+'/'+query string concat (collision-prone) -> struct key (backend.go ~372); taggerFor switch has a silent default -> make it exhaustive over queryKind (backend.go ~242); miner sentinelClass/constraintClass typed strings lack validation (miner.go ~62).
acceptance_criteria: OutlineEntry.Kind and miner classes are validated/typed; analyzer rule->lens mapping is data-driven; the tagger cache key is a struct; the queryKind switch is exhaustive; codenav tests pass.
close_reason: OutlineEntry.Kind now typed (treesitter.Kind, 8 consts, cast at gts.Tag boundary); analyzer 3 rule->lens switches replaced with data tables + LensName type via shared lensForPrefix; tagger cache key is a struct (taggerKey{grammar,kind}); taggerFor queryKind switch exhaustive (panics with clear msg on unknown); miner sentinelClass/constraintClass got Valid() guards preventing silent false matches. Build+test green for treesitter/analyzer/miner; integrator fixed tools_outline_test.go kind field type (treesitter.Kind) post-merge. Merged to main (db9f2d3).

title: Decompose funnel.Options god-struct into orthogonal config structs
description: funnel.Options (funnel.go ~297-424, 20+ fields) mixes budget knobs, per-role limits, feature flags, sandbox opts, and discovery filters. Split into orthogonal config structs (BudgetConfig, StageLimits, FeatureFlags, SandboxConfig, DiscoveryConfig) assembled by New(); stages take their own config type. Coordinate with the funnel/config sentinel bead.
acceptance_criteria: Options is composed from smaller single-concern structs; no behavior change; funnel tests pass; build green.
close_reason: funnel.Options decomposed into 4 orthogonal single-concern sub-structs (BudgetConfig/StageLimits/FeatureFlags/DiscoveryConfig) + top-level wiring (Progress/Repro/CodeNav/TranscriptDir/SandboxOpts); resolve() delegates to per-sub-struct resolve(); all 10 funnel internal files + construction sites (cli/bootstrap,cartography,scan; daemon; eval) + 25 test files migrated. Oracle GO: behavior-preserving, every default reproduced at identical thresholds, zero field misroutes. Full build+unit tests+lint green. Merged to main.

title: Decompose cli scan.go RunE god-function; ScanFlags; dedup --target; export --format enum; drop configPath global
description: cli scan.go RunE (~43-350) orchestrates context/store/repo/clients/progress/reproducer/seeding/funnel/repro-catchup/reporting in one closure (untestable). Extract openStore/openRepo/buildClients/buildReproHook/runScan stages and a ScanFlags struct (bootstrap.go FunnelOptionOverrides already shows the pattern). Also: dedup the --target flag across 6+ commands (addTargetFlag helper or shared flags struct); make export --format a typed/validated enum, not a hardcoded 'sarif' literal (export.go ~33); remove the package-global var configPath (root.go ~11).
acceptance_criteria: RunE delegates to named stage functions each independently callable; a ScanFlags struct carries scan flags; a single --target flag definition; export validates format via an enum; no package-global configPath; build+test green.
close_reason: (.6) PublishedIssue.State->IssueState, AgentUnit Role/Status/Strategy typed with consts; publish.go uses a tagged-union (publishCreate/Recover/Update/Close/Skip) so op-invalid field combos are unrepresentable; cli/progress use store.ScanKind consts. (.17) scan.go RunE decomposed into openStoreForScan/openRepoForScan/buildReproHookForScan/executeScan + ScanFlags; addTargetFlag dedups --target across 6 cmds; export --format is a validated ExportFormat enum; package-global configPath removed (configPathFromCmd). Oracle-reviewed: orchestration order + publish decisions verified byte-identical; caught an uncommitted-caller blocker (fixed: cmdOpenStore callers committed) + applied nits (orphan comment, switch default). Full build+unit tests+lint green. Merged to main.

title: funnel lens-name safety: typed LensName/registry, remove panic-on-missing, manifestations coverage test
description: Special lenses are looked up by name string with panic-on-missing (hypothesize.go diffIntentLens ~1213, crossLanguageBoundaryLens ~1297); manifestations[l.Name] and lensYields[name] silently return empty for a forgotten lens (silent precision loss). Introduce type LensName + consts for builtin names (or cache the Lens descriptors once at startup), replace the runtime name lookups, and add a test asserting every non-core BuiltinLens has manifestation rows.
acceptance_criteria: No panic-on-missing name lookups remain; lens references are typed/const; a coverage test fails if a non-core lens lacks manifestations; funnel tests pass.
close_reason: Removed panic-on-missing lens lookups: diffIntentLens/crossLanguageBoundaryLens now return package-level cached descriptors (builtinDiffIntentLens/builtinCrossLanguageBoundaryLens); added LensName + 11 builtin-name consts; added lens_coverage_test.go asserting every non-core BuiltinLens has manifestation rows + const/coreLensNames consistency. Merged to main.

title: Introduce minimal Store interface seam for testability
description: Every caller (funnel/cli/daemon) couples to the concrete *store.Store, forcing a real DB in tests. Export a minimal StoreReader/StoreWriter interface (start with 3-5 methods: GetFinding, UpsertFinding, GetScanRun, ...) that *Store satisfies; let tests use narrow doubles; grow the interface as tests demand. Optionally introduce typed ID wrappers (FindingID/ScanRunID) to stop cross-passing IDs - low priority, do only if cheap.
acceptance_criteria: A documented minimal Store interface exists and *Store implements it; at least one consumer test uses a double instead of a real DB; build+test green.

title: sandbox/deps narrow extractions: generic prefetch + cache-dir helpers (keep per-ecosystem duplication)
description: Audit verdict: the per-ecosystem strategy code (Go/Python/Rust/JS) is correctly divergent - DO NOT extract it behind an interface. Extract only the two genuine repeats: (1) the prefetch wrappers (newGoPrefetch/newPipPrefetch/newCargoPrefetch/newNPMPrefetch all share the sync.Once + hash-sentinel + online Exec + record pattern) into one generic prefetch helper; (2) fetchCacheDir/fetchPipCacheDir/fetchCargoCacheDir/fetchNPMCacheDir (differ only by an ecosystem-name string) into fetchEcosystemCacheDir. Make 'vendored' a DepStrategy const (deps.go ~99-130) and include it in ValidDepStrategy. Clarify ROMount.Shared (sandbox.go ~137-165) which overloads host-owned vs SELinux :Z label - express intent in the type or document clearly.
acceptance_criteria: A single generic prefetch helper and a single cache-dir helper replace the 4x duplicates; per-ecosystem resolve logic is unchanged; 'vendored' is a const present in ValidDepStrategy; ROMount.Shared intent is expressed in the type or clearly documented; sandbox integration tests (podman) pass.
notes: Integration tests: go test -tags integration ./internal/sandbox/ ./internal/repro/ (~25-30s, podman CLI backend, images alpine + golang:1.26-alpine already pulled). No LLM spend.
close_reason: (.7) sandbox.Ecosystem typed string (go/python/rust/js/cpp/bazel/unknown) adopted across repro+sandbox, no bare ecosystem comparisons remain. (.10) patchVerdictResult->patchVerdictKind and patchOutcome->patchOutcomeKind iota discriminants (contradictory states unrepresentable; neutral _ zero slot so a zero patchOutcome can't spuriously promote); VerdictReason+SmokeCategory typed consts; classifiers share centralized substrate (oracle: 3 classifiers genuinely distinct, FindingOutcome bools correctly hierarchical not exclusive). (.14) generic newPrefetchOnce (per-ecosystem Once preserved) + fetchEcosystemCacheDir replace 4x dups; DepStrategyVendored in ValidDepStrategy. Oracle GO; integration tests (sandbox 91s + repro 56s) pass; full unit suite green. Merged to main.

title: Agent tools: shared arg-validators, drop redundant toolError pre-wrap, relocate shared helpers
description: ~12 tools repeat json.Unmarshal + 'field required'/'line>=1'/path-traversal validation -> extract unmarshalArgs + requireField/requireLineNumber helpers in tool.go. Some tools pre-wrap errors in toolError() although runner.go already applies it uniformly (dead, divergent code) -> remove ALL pre-wrapping inside tool Run methods. Shared helpers readLine/symbolColumn/readFileLines live in tools_codenav.go but are used by 4 files -> move to a shared nav-helpers file. Pick one not-found convention (read_symbol's returns success-with-message is the model) and apply across tools. Unify the duplicated maxFileBytes const (codenav + grep) and the derivePkgDirectory logic (package_context x2).
acceptance_criteria: Shared arg parse/validate helpers used by all tools; zero toolError() calls inside tool Run methods; shared nav helpers relocated and documented; consistent not-found convention; agent tests pass; build green.
close_reason: Shared arg helpers (unmarshalArgs/requireField/requireLineNumber) adopted across all 12 tools; removed the 2 remaining toolError() pre-wraps in tool Run methods (find_usages/outline) — they now return ("",err) so runner.go wraps uniformly with IsError=true, fixing a latent IsError=false mislabel; nav helpers (readLine/symbolColumn/readFileLines) relocated to navhelpers.go; unified maxFileBytes->navMaxFileBytes and derivePkgDirectory. Internal to internal/agent, no API/ripple. Merged to main.

title: ingest.Change discriminant invariant; progress.Event variant typing
description: ingest.Change allows OldPath to be set on a non-rename Kind (change.go ~20) -> enforce via constructor or discriminated types (only ChangeRenamed carries OldPath). progress.Event (events.go ~114) is a flat struct with every optional field plus a Kind string discriminator, allowing invalid Kind/field combinations; Counts *Counts nil-vs-&Counts{} semantics are undocumented -> document + validate, or split per-Kind variant types.
acceptance_criteria: Change cannot carry OldPath unless renamed; Event field/Kind invariants are enforced or documented+validated and Counts semantics documented; ingest+progress tests pass.
close_reason: ingest.Change gets NewChange/NewRename constructors + Validate enforcing OldPath IFF ChangeRenamed (parser routed through them); progress.Event/Counts get per-Kind field docs, nil-vs-zero Counts semantics, and an advisory Event.Validate. Non-breaking (struct shapes unchanged, zero emitter ripple). Merged to main (3d2265e).

title: eval.Case sum type + drop derived CaseResult.Clean; daemon scheduler/cycleResult state
description: eval.Case{Scripted *ScriptedCase, Recorded *RecordedCase} allows both-nil/both-set -> tagged union or constructor enforcing exactly one. eval CaseResult.Clean is derived-but-stored (can diverge from len(Seeded)) -> compute on demand. daemon: scheduler state is spread across three loose time.Time deadlines + ad-hoc bool selection (run.go) and uses a magic 1<<62 sentinel to disable a timer; cycleResult{skipped bool, skipReason string} allows skipped=false with a non-empty reason -> group into a small state type and make skip a sum/option.
acceptance_criteria: eval.Case cannot be both/neither; CaseResult.Clean is not stored; cycleResult cannot carry a reason unless skipped; the disable-timer sentinel is replaced; daemon+eval tests pass.
close_reason: eval.Case: NewScriptedCase/NewRecordedCase constructors + CaseKind + Validate (rejects both-nil/both-set); CaseResult.Clean removed (now Clean() method over Seeded). daemon: schedule struct groups the 3 deadlines, nextBacklog *time.Time nil=disabled (replaces 1<<62 sentinel); earliest() preserves exact sweep>backlog>poll tie-break (integrator-fixed); cycleResult.skip *skipInfo so a reason cannot exist unless skipped. eval+daemon build/test/lint green. Merged to main.

title: Sum types for repro outcomes; typed verdict reasons; dedup classification
description: repro models mutually-exclusive outcomes as independent bools: patch.go patchVerdictResult{passed, envFailure, fixRejected}; promote.go FindingOutcome{Promoted, FixWitnessed, NeedsHuman}. verdict.reason (interpret.go) and SmokeVerdict.Category (verify_sandbox.go) are stringly-typed and the classification logic is duplicated across the two files. Convert outcomes to a sum type / single discriminated result; make reasons/categories typed enums; dedup classification into one path.
acceptance_criteria: Outcome structs cannot represent contradictory states (sum type or single enum field); verdict reason/category are typed consts; one classification path; repro tests incl. -tags integration pass.
close_reason: (.7) sandbox.Ecosystem typed string (go/python/rust/js/cpp/bazel/unknown) adopted across repro+sandbox, no bare ecosystem comparisons remain. (.10) patchVerdictResult->patchVerdictKind and patchOutcome->patchOutcomeKind iota discriminants (contradictory states unrepresentable; neutral _ zero slot so a zero patchOutcome can't spuriously promote); VerdictReason+SmokeCategory typed consts; classifiers share centralized substrate (oracle: 3 classifiers genuinely distinct, FindingOutcome bools correctly hierarchical not exclusive). (.14) generic newPrefetchOnce (per-ecosystem Once preserved) + fetchEcosystemCacheDir replace 4x dups; DepStrategyVendored in ValidDepStrategy. Oracle GO; integration tests (sandbox 91s + repro 56s) pass; full unit suite green. Merged to main.

title: Remove zero/negative-as-default sentinels in agent.Limits + BudgetPool
description: agent.Limits overloads zero=default and negative=unlimited for MaxIterations, TokenBudget, HistoryTokenBudget, and CacheReadWeight (<=0 silently corrected to 1.0 at the runner.go use site); BudgetPool is 'unlimited' when nil OR when limit<=0 (two encodings of one state) and Remaining() returns magic 1<<62. Replace with explicit optional/bounded representations (e.g. *int or a small Limit type with a constructor; an explicit Unlimited constructor for BudgetPool) so 0 is never ambiguous and no use-site correction is needed.
acceptance_criteria: No '<=0 means default/unlimited' correction at use sites; BudgetPool has a single unlimited representation; existing agent tests pass; build+test green.
close_reason: BudgetPool: nil *BudgetPool is now the SOLE unlimited representation (NewBudgetPool panics on limit<=0, unreachable in funnel which guards budget>0 / finderBudget,verifyBudget>0); dropped the limit<=0 dual-encoding and the 1<<62 magic (nil Remaining()->math.MaxInt64). Moved CacheReadWeight<=0->1.0 default from runner.overBudget use-site into Limits.resolve(); added named Unlimited=-1 const. agent+funnel tests green, lint clean. Merged to main.

title: Remove sentinel-overloaded budget knobs in funnel.Options + config.Budgets
description: funnel.Options.FinderBudgetShare (>=1 overloaded to disable reservation), CacheReadBudgetWeight, MaxParallel (0=default, <0=1); config.Budgets fields (FinderBudgetShare, CacheReadWeight, RequestTimeout) use 0-means-default which is then re-applied in funnel (duplicated default knowledge). Use *T optionals parsed once at config load (nil=default) or small bounded types; define each default exactly once. Coordinate with the funnel.Options decomposition bead.
acceptance_criteria: Default values are defined once; optional knobs use nil not 0; no >=1-disables-reservation overload remains; build+test green.

title: Shared Ecosystem type across sandbox/repro/ingest
description: Ecosystem (Go/Python/Rust/JS/Bazel) is first-class but modeled as bare unexported strings in repro/interpret.go (~34) and repro/patch.go (~513), branching on ecosystem=='bazel' etc. Define one type Ecosystem (in sandbox or domain) and use it in repro; align with sandbox.DepStrategy / ingest.Language conventions.
acceptance_criteria: A single Ecosystem type is used by repro (and sandbox where applicable); no bare ecosystem string comparisons remain; build+test green.
close_reason: (.7) sandbox.Ecosystem typed string (go/python/rust/js/cpp/bazel/unknown) adopted across repro+sandbox, no bare ecosystem comparisons remain. (.10) patchVerdictResult->patchVerdictKind and patchOutcome->patchOutcomeKind iota discriminants (contradictory states unrepresentable; neutral _ zero slot so a zero patchOutcome can't spuriously promote); VerdictReason+SmokeCategory typed consts; classifiers share centralized substrate (oracle: 3 classifiers genuinely distinct, FindingOutcome bools correctly hierarchical not exclusive). (.14) generic newPrefetchOnce (per-ecosystem Once preserved) + fetchEcosystemCacheDir replace 4x dups; DepStrategyVendored in ValidDepStrategy. Oracle GO; integration tests (sandbox 91s + repro 56s) pass; full unit suite green. Merged to main.

title: Typed status/state enums: IssueState, AgentRole/Status, reuse ScanKind
description: store.Finding.Status is already a good typed enum; apply the same pattern to its stringly-typed siblings: store.published.go PublishedIssue.State - a real 4-state machine (pending/open/closing/closed) checked via magic strings at cli/publish.go ~344/349/369/374 - becomes type IssueState with consts; store.agentunits.go Status/Role/Strategy become typed; and cli/progress reuse store.ScanKind consts instead of raw 'sweep'/'targeted' strings (cli/status.go ~89, cli/scan.go ~222). Also split cli publish.go syncAction/publishAction structs whose fields are valid only for specific ops into per-op types (or a tagged union) so invalid field combos are unrepresentable.
acceptance_criteria: PublishedIssue.State, AgentUnit.Status/Role typed with consts; publish.go compares typed consts not string literals; ScanKind string literals gone from cli/progress; publish action structs cannot represent op-invalid field combos; build+test green.
close_reason: (.6) PublishedIssue.State->IssueState, AgentUnit Role/Status/Strategy typed with consts; publish.go uses a tagged-union (publishCreate/Recover/Update/Close/Skip) so op-invalid field combos are unrepresentable; cli/progress use store.ScanKind consts. (.17) scan.go RunE decomposed into openStoreForScan/openRepoForScan/buildReproHookForScan/executeScan + ScanFlags; addTargetFlag dedups --target across 6 cmds; export --format is a validated ExportFormat enum; package-global configPath removed (configPathFromCmd). Oracle-reviewed: orchestration order + publish decisions verified byte-identical; caught an uncommitted-caller blocker (fixed: cmdOpenStore callers committed) + applied nits (orphan comment, switch default). Full build+unit tests+lint green. Merged to main.

title: Unify the two SARIF emitters; fix Region optionality
description: Two independent hand-rolled SARIF 2.1.0 emitters exist: internal/report/sarif.go (SARIFLog, optional *SARIFRegion) and internal/sarif/sarif.go (Document, non-pointer Region). Make internal/sarif the canonical model; have report build on it (or remove one). Fix the divergence where internal/sarif emits Region even when Line==0 (false startLine:0): use *Region set only when Line>0, matching report's correct behavior. Use domain.Tier.Level() / domain.Severity for level mapping.
acceptance_criteria: One SARIF model; the report path delegates to internal/sarif; Region is omitted when there is no line; existing SARIF tests and cli/export_test pass; build+test green.
close_reason: Unified two SARIF emitters onto internal/sarif (canonical); deleted report's 9 duplicate SARIF types; report.BuildSARIF/SARIF() are thin adapters (public signature unchanged). Fixed Region bug: PhysicalLocation.Region is now *Region/omitempty set only when Line>0 (no more false startLine:0). Level via domain.Tier.Level()/domain.Severity. sarif+report+cli tests green, lint clean, golden parity preserved. Merged to main.

title: Migrate Confidence callers to domain.Confidence
description: Replace bare-string confidence: funnel.Candidate.Confidence, helpers.normalizeConfidence, and the triage gate that drops candidates on c.Confidence == 'low' (triage_streaming.go). Gate on the typed domain.Confidence value.
acceptance_criteria: funnel uses domain.Confidence; the low-confidence drop compares a typed value; build+test green.
close_reason: Migrated Tier(int)->domain.Tier, Severity(string)->domain.Severity, Candidate.Confidence(string)->domain.Confidence across store/funnel/report/cli/sarif/config/daemon/eval. Deleted all duplicated mappers (tierName/tierLabel/tierLevel/severityRank/validSeverities); call sites route through domain methods. Fixed sortFindings sign-flip (Rank higher=more-severe, si>sj keeps critical-first) and sandbox gate (!AtLeast(min)). Resolved tier-0: TierFixWitnessed now strongest (BaseConfidence 0.90, Level error) — pinned by domain/store/sarif tests. Oracle-reviewed (GO, no correctness defects); gofmt+golangci-lint clean; full unit suite green. Merged to main.

title: Migrate Severity callers to domain.Severity
description: Replace bare-string severity across: store.Finding.Severity, config.validSeverities + config.Verify.SandboxMinSeverity (config.go ~234/~315), funnel.severityRank (funnel.go ~1145) + funnel.Candidate.Severity + helpers.normalizeSeverity, and the severity->SARIF level mapping. Parse to domain.Severity at the finder/ingest boundary; downstream uses the typed value and Rank(). Watch for a config<->store import cycle (domain is the leaf that avoids it).
acceptance_criteria: A single domain.Severity is used everywhere; config validates via domain.ParseSeverity; the duplicate validSeverities map and severityRank switch are gone; build+test green.
close_reason: Migrated Tier(int)->domain.Tier, Severity(string)->domain.Severity, Candidate.Confidence(string)->domain.Confidence across store/funnel/report/cli/sarif/config/daemon/eval. Deleted all duplicated mappers (tierName/tierLabel/tierLevel/severityRank/validSeverities); call sites route through domain methods. Fixed sortFindings sign-flip (Rank higher=more-severe, si>sj keeps critical-first) and sandbox gate (!AtLeast(min)). Resolved tier-0: TierFixWitnessed now strongest (BaseConfidence 0.90, Level error) — pinned by domain/store/sarif tests. Oracle-reviewed (GO, no correctness defects); gofmt+golangci-lint clean; full unit suite green. Merged to main.

title: Migrate all Tier callers to domain.Tier; resolve tier-0 confidence
description: Replace store.Finding.Tier int with domain.Tier and DELETE the duplicated int->meaning mappings: tierName (report/report.go ~110), tierLabel (cli/prcomments.go ~96), tierLevel (sarif/sarif.go ~92), the tier switch inside store.findingConfidence (~109), and the funnel-private constants tierVerified=2 / tierSuspected=3 (funnel/persist.go, funnel/helpers.go). All call sites use domain.Tier methods (Label/Level/Confidence). Encode the Tier-0 decision made in the foundation bead.
acceptance_criteria: No int tier-literal mapping remains (search confirms only domain.Tier methods); store.Finding.Tier is domain.Tier; funnel uses the shared consts not private ones; tier-0 ordering is tested; go build ./... and go test ./... green.
close_reason: Migrated Tier(int)->domain.Tier, Severity(string)->domain.Severity, Candidate.Confidence(string)->domain.Confidence across store/funnel/report/cli/sarif/config/daemon/eval. Deleted all duplicated mappers (tierName/tierLabel/tierLevel/severityRank/validSeverities); call sites route through domain methods. Fixed sortFindings sign-flip (Rank higher=more-severe, si>sj keeps critical-first) and sandbox gate (!AtLeast(min)). Resolved tier-0: TierFixWitnessed now strongest (BaseConfidence 0.90, Level error) — pinned by domain/store/sarif tests. Oracle-reviewed (GO, no correctness defects); gofmt+golangci-lint clean; full unit suite green. Merged to main.

title: Foundation: define centralized domain types (Tier, Severity, Confidence)
description: Create a leaf package internal/domain (imports only stdlib, so store AND config can import it without an import cycle) defining: type Tier uint8 with consts TierFixWitnessed=0, TierReproduced=1, TierVerified=2, TierSuspected=3 and methods Label() string, Level() string (SARIF level), Confidence() float64; type Severity string with consts SeverityCritical/High/Medium/Low plus Rank() int and ParseSeverity/normalize; type Confidence string with consts ConfidenceHigh/Medium/Low plus Rank() and normalize. This bead ONLY introduces the types + tests; no caller migration (separate beads). It locks the stable target so the other agents working concurrently can build against it immediately. Decision to verify and encode: the intended ordering/confidence for Tier 0 (Fix-witnessed) - today store.findingConfidence folds tier 0 into the suspected/default 0.20 bucket; choose the correct value and pin it with a test.
design: Leaf package internal/domain chosen over putting the types in store, to avoid an import cycle: config needs Severity and store may import config. domain imports only the standard library; every other package imports domain. This mirrors the llm package's discipline of a small, dependency-free core.
acceptance_criteria: internal/domain package exists with the three types, their consts and methods; table tests cover every const incl. Tier 0 Label/Level/Confidence and unknown-value normalization for Severity/Confidence; go build ./... and go test ./internal/domain/ are green; no existing caller is changed by this bead.
close_reason: Landed internal/domain leaf package (doc.go, tier.go, severity.go, confidence.go + domain_test.go, all 7 tests pass, go build ./... green). Decisions: (1) Tier uint8 with TierFixWitnessed=0..TierSuspected=3; Label/Level/BaseConfidence preserve the EXACT pre-refactor numbers (incl. the tier-0 quirk: BaseConfidence 0.20 and SARIF level 'note', the weakest, despite fix-witnessed being semantically strongest) so the upcoming migrations are pure refactors with zero behavior change; correcting tier-0 is left as a deliberate product decision for bugbot-0nc.2 and pinned by a test. (2) Severity.Rank uses higher=more-severe (critical=4..low=1, unknown=0, matching report.SortFindings) plus an AtLeast helper so call sites never juggle rank direction (funnel's opposite-direction severityRank migrates to AtLeast). (3) ParseSeverity/ParseConfidence return (value, ok) and bake in NO default, because the three current normalize sites use different defaults (medium / high / low) that must stay at the call site. Leaf package chosen to avoid a store<->config import cycle.

title: Type-design hardening: centralize domain vocabulary, make invalid states unrepresentable
description: Code-quality audit (16-way read-only fan-out, 2026-06-19) found the system's core domain vocabulary modeled as bare int/string and re-validated or re-mapped in many places, with semantics already drifting (e.g. a 4th confidence tier T0 'Fix-witnessed' that store.findingConfidence does not handle, folding it into the lowest bucket). The internal/llm package is the exemplary standard: minimal interfaces, typed sentinel errors, composable decorators. This epic orients the whole codebase to that standard before it rots: centralize domain enums (tier/severity/confidence/status/ecosystem), parse-don't-validate at boundaries, replace zero/negative-as-default sentinels with optional/bounded types, convert mutually-exclusive-bool structs to sum types, unify the two duplicate SARIF emitters, and decompose two god-shapes (cli scan RunE, funnel.Options). Children are independently workable except the four migrations that depend on the foundational domain-types bead. Full audit lives in the originating session transcript.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads closed; no duplicated tier/severity/confidence mapping or validation remains (single typed source of truth); the two SARIF emitters are unified; go build ./... and go test ./... are green; no new stringly-typed domain fields introduced.
notes: Cross-cutting headliners (verified against source): (1) store.Finding.Tier is bare int; int->meaning duplicated in report.tierName, prcomments.tierLabel, sarif.tierLevel, store.findingConfidence + funnel-private tierVerified/tierSuspected. (2) Severity bare string in store/config/funnel/sarif. (3) Confidence bare string; triage gates on c.Confidence=='low'. (4) Finding.Status is typed (good) but PublishedIssue.State + AgentUnit.Status/Role are not. (5) Two hand-rolled SARIF emitters (internal/report/sarif.go vs internal/sarif/sarif.go) diverged on Region optionality. Exemplary target = internal/llm.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: Orchestration plan (2026-06-19): dependency analysis shows the domain migration (.2/.3/.4) is NOT package-isolated — Finding.Tier/Severity are read across store/funnel/report/cli/sarif/config/daemon/eval/repro, so it must land as a keystone BEFORE other domain-touching clusters branch. Wave A (parallel, off main): C1=domain keystone(.2+.3+.4), C3=agent(.8+.13), C5=ingest/progress(.12), C6=codenav(.19) — C3/C5/C6 live in packages C1 doesn't touch. Wave B (off post-C1 main): C2=repro+sandbox(.7+.10+.14), C4=cli+status(.6+.17), C7=eval+daemon(.11), SARIF(.5), FUNNEL-OPT(.9+.18), FUNNEL-LENS(.15). Wave C: STORE-IFACE(.16)+CLEANUP(.20). Each cluster: own git worktree, oracle review, sequential merge to main. TIER-0 DECISION for .2: correct TierFixWitnessed to strongest (BaseConfidence 0.90, Level error) — the lone inconsistency vs how tier-0 is treated elsewhere.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: Refined wave schedule (file-overlap verified): WaveA(parallel,off main)=C1 domain[.2/.3/.4 | wt domain], C-TOOLS[.13 internal/agent only | wt agent], C5 ingest-progress[.12 non-breaking constructors+validation | wt ingest], C6 codenav[.19 analyzer+miner+treesitter | wt codenav]. .8(agent budget) is a 2nd keystone (ripples to funnel/repro/cli/eval) -> WaveB. WaveB(off post-C1)=C-BUDGET[.8], C4 cli-status[.6/.17], C-SARIF[.5], C-FUNNEL-LENS[.15]. WaveC(off post-B)=C2 repro-sandbox[.7/.10/.14], C7 eval-daemon[.11], C-FUNNEL-OPT[.9/.18]. WaveD=STORE-IFACE[.16], CLEANUP[.20]. Each wave's clusters touch mutually-disjoint files; sequential waves branch off prior merged main.

title: openai-compatible: downgrade object-valued additionalProperties to boolean on the wire
description: MiniMax (and other openai-compatible structured-output backends) run a strict schema type-checker over response_format/tool parameters that accepts ONLY a boolean additionalProperties and rejects the JSON-Schema subschema form (e.g. {"type":"string"} for free-form string maps) with HTTP 400 (error 2013, 'Mismatch type bool with value object') BEFORE any tokens are generated. In a real services-runtime session both reproducer agents died infra_error/0-tokens at iteration 1: planSchema (repro/agent.go) and patchSchema (repro/patch.go) ship via response_format, and the sandbox_exec tool (agent/tools_sandbox_exec.go) ships via tool parameters, all carrying object-valued additionalProperties.
design: Systemic over per-schema edit: a recursive coerceBoolAdditionalProperties pass in the openai adapter centralizes the openai-compatible wire quirk and auto-covers future map schemas. Gated by a requireBoolAdditionalProps flag set true only in the registry openai-compatible branch (the factory is the legitimate place to branch on provider type). Downgrade to true (not false) keeps the map open; false would reject all dynamic keys. Lossless because the adapter mutates only a freshly-unmarshalled wire copy; runjson.go validates model output against the original schema bytes.
acceptance_criteria: openai adapter buildParams downgrades every object/array additionalProperties to boolean true in BOTH tool parameters and response_format schema for the openai-compatible provider; first-party openai is unchanged; the local RunJSON validator still enforces value types against the original schema (lossless); unit + wire (httptest) tests cover the transform and both providers; go build/vet/test ./internal/llm pass.
close_reason: Fixed via PR #80 (squash-merged to main as f0bfd0f). openai adapter downgrades object/array-valued additionalProperties to boolean true for openai-compatible providers (response_format + tool params), gated by requireBoolAdditionalProps; first-party OpenAI unchanged. Schema-aware + lossless (runjson.go validates against original schema). All CI green (build/vet/test -race, golangci-lint v2, CodeQL). Live MiniMax verification still pending (no API key in dev env).

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text:
Fixed on branch fix/d80-addlprops-bool (worktree ../bugbot-wt/d80), commit 0c512d7, pushed to origin.

Implementation: openai adapter now downgrades object/array-valued additionalProperties to boolean true for BOTH tool parameters and response_format, gated by requireBoolAdditionalProps (set only in the registry's openai-compatible branch). Schema-aware walk skips property NAMES and enum/const literal data. Lossless — runjson.go still validates value types against the original schema.

Verification (go test ./internal/llm + repro/agent, all pass):
- TestCoerceBoolAdditionalProperties: 6 subtests incl. property-named-additionalProperties and enum/const object literals left intact, bool false preserved.
- TestSchemaCompat_OpenAICompatible_DowngradesResponseFormat / _DowngradesToolParameters: wire body carries additionalProperties:true (root false preserved) for the exact planSchema/sandbox_exec files-map shape.
- TestSchemaCompat_OpenAI_PreservesSubschema: first-party OpenAI unchanged (blast-radius lock).
- TestSchemaCompat_Registry_OpenAICompatibleEnablesDowngrade: full NewClient factory path.

CAVEAT: no live MiniMax key in this env, so not verified against the real API. Reasoning that the fix is sufficient: error 2013 is a schema TYPE check and additionalProperties was the only keyword we send with the wrong type (object vs bool); other keywords (minProperties/minItems/minLength/enum) carry type-correct values. If MiniMax later rejects another keyword, that is a separate follow-up.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text:
LIVE VERIFICATION against api.minimax.io (MiniMax-M3) — caveat lifted. Two minimal calls via llm.NewClient:
- RAW path (openai provider type, no downgrade): reproduced the exact original failure — HTTP 400 'Mismatch type bool with value object ... additionalProperties:{type:string} (2013)'. Confirms the diagnosis against the real endpoint.
- FIXED path (openai-compatible, downgrade on): HTTP 200, stop=end_turn, in=188/out=28, model returned schema-valid {"files":{"a.txt":"hi"}}.
Fix is verified end-to-end live. Billing footprint negligible (one rejected request + one ~216-token completion).

title: OpenAI-compatible adapter drops reasoning-model output (MiniMax M2.7 finders unparseable)
description: When the finder role runs on a thinking/reasoning model via the openai-compatible provider (e.g. MiniMax M2.7), 123/126 finder agents produced no parseable output and the scan aborted as unreliable. Hypothesis: internal/llm/openai.go toResponse() reads only choice.Message.Content and ignores reasoning_content; reasoning models either put the answer in reasoning_content (leaving Content empty/partial) or inline <think>...</think> into Content, which the finder parser cannot handle.
design: REVISED after live hang (goroutine dump on commit 949b755). The PRIMARY defect is a missing per-request timeout on the LLM client path — proven by goroutine 135 stuck in openai.go:67 Complete -> openai-go ChatCompletionService.New -> http2 roundTrip with no deadline, wedging hypothesize.go:514 wg.Wait() for 4.5min. openai.go newOpenAIAdapter uses option.WithMaxRetries(0) and the default http.Client (no Timeout); finder goroutines pass the scan ctx with no per-call deadline. ANY slow/stalled provider request hangs the whole run; raising max_tokens to 32768 just made it bite reliably. The original reasoning-model token issue is SECONDARY and was reverted (commit b50fa3d restores DefaultMaxOutputTokens=8192). Correct fix order: (1) bound every LLM Complete call with a per-request timeout (context.WithTimeout in the client wrapper or a configurable http.Client.Timeout), so a stalled request fails fast and surfaces as a finder error instead of a hang — must cover finder/refuter/arbiter and the retry/recording wrappers; default generous (e.g. 5-10min) and configurable; (2) ONLY THEN reintroduce the configurable max_output_tokens (do NOT raise the default to 32768 without the timeout in place; keep default 8192 and let operators opt into higher). Pre-existing miss the reviewer flagged and I shipped over — do not repeat.
acceptance_criteria: Root cause confirmed against a real raw M2.7 response shape. Fix lets a finder running on MiniMax M2.7 produce parseable output (reasoning_content surfaced and/or <think> blocks stripped before parsing). Adapter change covered by a test. go build/vet/test and gofmt clean. Reviewed by a fresh reviewer and merged to main.
notes: TIMEOUT FIX MERGED to main (commit ce121c8 -> merge f6f3fc7). Added RequestTimeout (default 5m per attempt) to retryClient: each inner Complete call runs under context.WithTimeout(parentCtx, 5m); a stall fails as child-only DeadlineExceeded (parent stays nil), classified retryable, so it retries instead of hanging wg.Wait(). Parent cancellation still aborts without retry. registry.go wraps all production adapters via WithRetry+DefaultRetryConfig so every role is covered. Independently reviewed APPROVE WITH NITS; -race clean on llm/agent/funnel. The hang root cause is now fixed. The original reasoning-model token-budget work remains REVERTED and is the remaining open scope on this bead (reintroduce configurable max_output_tokens, default still 8192, now safe because requests can no longer hang).
close_reason: Root cause CONFIRMED live against MiniMax-M2.7 (https://api.minimax.io/v1, 2026-06-17): reasoning is an inline <think>...</think> block at the START of choices[0].message.content (message keys: content/role/name/audio_content — there is NO reasoning_content field), followed by the answer in a markdown json fence; the <think> body can itself contain a nested code fence. The agent layer ALREADY tolerates this: internal/agent/runjson.go parseInto runs stripFences(stripThinkBlocks(text)) before unmarshal (added in b647433, present before this bead's work). The actual breakage was the request hang, fixed by the already-merged per-call LLM timeout. The adapter-level <think> strip I briefly merged was REDUNDANT with parseInto (and non-anchored, so it risked corrupting a <think> substring inside a JSON string value) — REVERTED in b685317. Added regression test TestRunJSON_MiniMaxM27Reasoning (ce40738) using the real M2.7 shape. Verified end-to-end: live conformance probe (go test -tags live -run TestLive against MiniMax-M2.7) passes — plain completion, tool round-trip (clean read_file tool call + parseable args), and usage recording all green. full gofmt/vet/build/test clean. reasoning_content surfacing (bugbot-3b0) is N/A for MiniMax (no such field); configurable max_output_tokens remains operator-held.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Remaining ci0 scope (reintroduce configurable max_output_tokens) should NOT ship until bugbot-3b0's root-cause question is settled — token-raise is a mask, not a fix (per [[ship-only-after-confirming-root-cause]]). bugbot-3b0 is now gated on bugbot-hp4 (instrument no-parse failures) so we confirm the real M2.7 response shape from captured data instead of SDK-struct guessing.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Merged the shippable defensive slice: openai adapter toResponse now strips inline <think>/<thinking> CoT blocks (precompiled (?is)<think(?:ing)?>.*?</think(?:ing)?> regex, non-greedy, unterminated-tag-safe) before the finder parser sees the text, handling the inline-CoT failure shape (commit 921dca8, merged --no-ff). NOT closing: acceptance item 'root cause confirmed against a real raw M2.7 response shape' is unmet (no MINIMAX_API_KEY available — that live confirmation is bugbot-mnr); the separate reasoning_content/reasoning_details field shape is bugbot-3b0 (gated on hp4, now merged); the configurable max_output_tokens work remains operator-held per the 2026-06-13 comment. Residual ci0 scope is fully tracked by mnr + 3b0; this bead stays open until live confirmation settles which shape M2.7 actually uses.

title: Budget accounting charges cached input at full weight — cache-heavy real scans exhaust per_cycle_tokens at a fraction of actual cost
description: DOGFOOD FINDING (bugbot-9bd): scanning bugbot's own 27-file core hit the 6M per_cycle_tokens ceiling and produced ZERO findings. Breakdown: input=6.12M of which 5.19M (85%) were CACHE READS; output only 59k. The budget counts InputTokens inclusive of cached tokens at full weight (funnel doc comment is explicit), but cache reads bill at ~10%. So a heavily-cached run — which is the steady-state once prompt prefixes stabilize — exhausts the token budget ~5-6x faster than its real dollar cost warrants, starving later finder units. The hard-stop hit after ~10 of ~24 (lens,chunk) units, leaving most lenses unrun and 0 candidates. Fix options: (a) weight cache_read at a configurable fraction (default ~0.1) in budget accounting, or (b) track budget in estimated cost not raw tokens. (a) is smaller. This is the #1 blocker for real-repo runs.
acceptance_criteria: Budget accounting discounts cache_read_tokens by a configurable weight (default making cached reads count ~0.1x); a re-run of the bugbot-core dogfood scan completes all lenses on all chunks within a comparable cost budget and produces findings; existing budget tests updated; per_cycle/per_day semantics documented as cost-weighted; eval suite unchanged.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #18 (squash 40f8ae5). Cache reads now weighted (default 0.1) in ALL budget gates — per-turn BudgetPool, per-run Runner.overBudget, funnel soft/hard degradation (new weighted accumulator; raw totalTokens kept for honest ledger/status), and daemon per-day (SpendTotals.Chargeable). config.Budgets.CacheReadWeight + BUGBOT_BUDGETS_CACHE_READ_WEIGHT + template; 1.0 = raw. Review caught the must-fix: soft/hard + per-day gates were still raw and would have reintroduced the bug at a different boundary. Dogfood re-run at same 6M budget got 7+ finder units in with ZERO budget-degradation (pre-fix: degraded by this point, 0 findings) — confirms the fix. NOTE: budget now reflects real cost, but raw token SCALE (30M+ input on 27 files) remains the bigger issue, tracked separately as bugbot-3nf.

title: Impact-assessment sweep: low-budget end-of-scan reachability/severity re-rank
description: PROPOSED (user, 2026-06-19) as the next step in weighting finding impact: a SEPARATE, low-budget impact-assessment agent sweep that runs at the END of a scan, after the finder/verifier/refuter stages (which work well). The 2026-06-19 services-runtime C++ dogfood run shipped ~14/31 findings in dead/uncalled code (add_effect 0 callers; Surface 2-arg ctor; JSON::write; RegisterableFuncType::bind callable only from its ctor; count_frames; PosixPoller; split) or platform code not compiled on the Linux target (WindowsFdSet under #ifdef _WIN32; macOS toolchain) at medium/high severity. The funnel verifies 'is this a real defect' but never asks 'does this code run, and who controls its inputs', so severity is defect-class-weighted not impact-weighted.
design: SEAM: add the stage in internal/funnel/run.go AFTER verifyFanout.wait() (~line 676) operating on the run's persisted `allFindings`, BEFORE finalize/return -- this gives parity for both `bugbot scan` (oneshot run()) and the daemon (Sweep/Targeted -> run()), exactly where in-run repro already hooks. Severity is a bare string on Candidate/Finding (funnel.go:621, set by the finder); the sweep rewrites it and stores a rationale. COST (M3 is input-dominated ~130:1, no prompt caching): compute reachability DETERMINISTICALLY first -- caller count via lsp references / grep, dead-code detection, test-only path, and build-tag/#ifdef platform gating -- so most findings are classified with ZERO LLM tokens; feed only the compact (finding, caller-facts) tuples to a SINGLE batched cheap-model adjudication that emits {severity, rationale}. RELATIONSHIP: this is the end-of-scan-sweep implementation of the goal diagnosed in bugbot-bar (which proposed reachability/trust-boundary SEATS inside the per-finding verifier/arbiter). Recommend the sweep over per-seat (cheaper: deterministic batch vs per-finding LLM seat); supersede-vs-keep bugbot-bar flagged for the user. Will `bd dep add bugbot-bar <this>`.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A new stage re-ranks each surviving finding's severity using a reachability/impact analysis and records the rationale in the verdict detail. (2) Replaying the services-runtime C++ run, the dead/uncalled/platform-gated findings (#7,#8,#9,#15,#20,#21,#22,#24,#25,#26,#27,#28, macOS #1) drop to low/latent with caller-trace rationale, while genuinely-reachable findings (#2/3 every-frame, #5/6 UAF, #13/14 lighting, #19 per-tile leak, #29 startup leak, #18 identity poison) retain high. (3) The sweep's added spend is a small fraction of a scan (dominated by deterministic analysis, at most one batched LLM adjudication call). (4) [ABSORBED FROM bugbot-bar -- Go dogfood corpus, a second-ecosystem regression check] Replaying the FIRST (Go) dogfood run, severity drops with recorded reachability/trust-boundary rationale: #52 (lsp Manager Close race) -> low/medium (every query goroutine is joined by wg.Wait before Funnel.Close; worst case one orphaned gopls dies on stdin EOF at process exit); #57 (ChangedFiles omits --end-of-options) -> low (every dash-capable input is the local operator's own CLI flag -- PR SHAs are 40-hex, git forbids dash-leading branch names -- no privilege boundary crossed); #55 (repro prompt-injection) -> info. (5) The analysis answers BOTH 'can the program reach this state' AND 'who controls the inputs' (trust boundary), and the stored severity rationale cites it.
notes: Trust-boundary axis (who controls the inputs) belongs here: e.g. TextureAtlas underflow #16 is only reachable with attacker-controlled atlas dims, but the sole call site passes constants -- impact LOW today. Keep the deterministic classifier conservative: 'dead' must mean zero non-test callers AND not an exported library entrypoint intended for external use. SUPERSEDES bugbot-bar (severity calibration): bar proposed reachability/trust-boundary SEATS inside the per-finding verifier/arbiter; this replaces that with a cheaper end-of-scan deterministic-classify + batched-adjudication sweep. Complements bugbot-aud (execute-the-claim: refuters are strong at 'is the code shaped as claimed', weak at dynamic semantics / threat-model context).

title: funnel: persist in-flight candidates (WAL) so interrupted runs are not lost
description: Interrupting a oneshot (or daemon) scan permanently loses every hypothesis (funnel.Candidate) that had not reached a terminal fate. The streaming funnel (bugbot-280) made findings, per-unit coverage, and the scan_runs row durable, but candidates between hypothesize and verify live only in in-memory channels (candCh/verCh in run.go) and in-flight verify goroutines. On ctx cancel emit drops them (run.go:508) and the goroutines unwind. Compounding it: TouchScanCoverage already stamped those files at finder-unit completion (hypothesize.go:695), so the next sweep deprioritizes them (Group 2 stalest-first) and the candidates are never recovered. The expensive finder work is thrown away.
design: Write-ahead log of candidates. New store table pending_candidates (migration 009): one row per finder-emitted candidate, full Candidate fidelity (lens/file/line/title/description/severity/evidence/confidence/corroborating_lenses + scan_run_id + commit). (1) PERSIST at the finder emit site (hypothesize.go ~675), batched per finder unit, before candidates enter the volatile channel pipeline — same per-unit durability discipline as the coverage stamp. Candidate gains a PendingID field carrying the row PK. (2) DELETE at every terminal fate: triage drops (low-conf/out-of-scope/dup/suppressed) + cluster merge (triage_streaming.go), and verify survived/killed/orphaned (verify_stream.go). A clean run empties the WAL; only an interrupt leaves rows. Delete runs on the run ctx so a cancelled run does NOT delete in-flight rows (they persist). (3) REPLAY at run start (run.go): ListPendingCandidates from prior runs, emit into the same triage/verify pipeline BEFORE fresh hypothesize, re-anchored to the current snapshot — skips re-hypothesize, verifier re-checks against current code (stale candidates get killed; precision-safe). Always-on (no config knob). Crash-safe (WAL survives SIGKILL, not just SIGINT). Stats.Resumed counts replayed candidates. No retention prune needed: replay self-cleans out-of-scope rows via the triage drop+delete.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Interrupt a sweep after a finder emits candidates but before verify completes: pending_candidates table has the un-verified candidate rows (test via gated verifier + watchdog cancel). 2) A second run on the same store replays those rows: they are verified and persisted as findings, and the pending_candidates table is drained to zero. 3) A clean (uninterrupted) run leaves pending_candidates empty at finalize (every candidate deleted at terminal fate). 4) Persist/delete/replay are best-effort: a store error is f.note-logged and never aborts the scan. 5) store unit tests for AddPendingCandidates (batch)/ListPendingCandidates/DeletePendingCandidate incl. empty-id no-op. 6) make test green.
close_reason: Implemented a candidate write-ahead log (pending_candidates table, migration 009). Finder-emitted candidates are persisted per-unit BEFORE entering the volatile channel pipeline (hypothesize.go) and deleted at every terminal fate — triage drops/merge (triage_streaming.go) and verify survived/killed/orphaned (verify_stream.go via Funnel.deletePending). run() replays surviving rows from prior interrupted runs into triage/verify before fresh hypothesize (pendingToCandidate), re-anchored to the current snapshot so stale candidates are dropped/killed precision-safely. Always-on (correctness, no config knob); crash-safe (survives SIGKILL, not just SIGINT). Stats.Resumed counts replayed candidates and is surfaced in the CLI scan summary. Delete runs on the run ctx so an interrupted run keeps its in-flight rows. Tests: store round-trip + empty-id/empty-batch no-ops; funnel interrupt->resume (candidate survives interrupt, next run verifies it, WAL drains), clean-run-drains-WAL. Full suite (18 pkg), go vet, gofmt all green.

title: Deep schema enforcement at every LLM phase boundary (strengthen jwd's shallow validation)
description: Follow-up to closed bugbot-jwd/gd3/w88. Those wired native structured-output (ResponseSchema) and a HARNESS-side shape check, but the harness check (agent.validateRootShape) is deliberately shallow: it only verifies the root JSON type and top-level required keys. It does NOT recurse into array items or nested object properties, and ignores enum/minItems/minProperties/minLength/minimum/additionalProperties. So when a provider's StructuredOutput capability is OFF (the dogfood MiniMax-M3 openai-compatible path is opt-in), the ONLY real enforcement is a typed json.Unmarshal — which silently tolerates bad enums (severity='blocker'), missing NESTED required fields (a candidate with no evidence), empty required strings, and empty files maps (coerced/defaulted downstream, e.g. normalizeSeverity maps unknown->'medium'). Every LLM phase output is the next phase's input, so a loose output schema = loose input contract. Make the schema strong and well-defined: a full (subset) JSON-Schema validator applied to the parsed body at the single RunJSON chokepoint, plus tightened schema definitions.
design: Hand-rolled validator over a closed in-repo schema set (5 schemas) instead of a new JSON-Schema library dependency: zero new deps (project values minimal deps), validates exactly the constructs used, emits repair-prompt-tuned path-qualified errors. Parse schema per call (matches existing validateRootShape; negligible vs LLM latency). Root-level error phrasing preserved verbatim ('root JSON type ...', 'root object missing required field ...') so existing tests and any caller classification stay stable. Behavior change: strict enum now triggers a repair round-trip where normalizeSeverity/normalizeConfidence previously silently coerced — intended (precision lever); bounded to one repair; absent entirely when native StructuredOutput cap is on (grammar prevents violations). additionalProperties:false enforced for parity with native decoding.
acceptance_criteria: agent.validateSchema replaces validateRootShape and recursively enforces type (incl. integer vs number), required (nested), properties, additionalProperties (false rejects unknowns; schema validates values), items, enum, minItems, minProperties, minLength, maxLength, minimum with path-qualified errors; RunJSON routes any violation through the existing single repair round-trip and re-validates the repair; schema defs tightened where genuinely invariant (planSchema files minProperties:1, required free-text minLength:1, candidate line minimum:1) WITHOUT adding constraints that reject the valid 'found nothing' empty-candidates output; go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/funnel/... ./internal/repro/... ./internal/llm/... green; scripted eval gate still precision=1.000.
close_reason: Replaced agent.validateRootShape (shallow: root type + top-level required only) with a deep recursive validateSchema enforcing the full JSON-Schema subset the phase schemas use — type/integer, nested required, properties, additionalProperties (false-reject + subschema-validate), items, enum, minItems/minProperties/minLength/maxLength/minimum — with path-qualified errors routed through RunJSON's existing single repair round-trip (re-validated on repair). Hand-rolled, zero new deps, over the closed 5-schema set. Tightened the schemas themselves: candidate line minimum:1 + minLength:1 on file/title/description/evidence, refuter reasoning minLength:1, plan files minProperties:1 + expect minLength:1, patch summary minLength:1, cartographer summary minLength:1 — deliberately NOT adding minItems to candidates (empty=found-nothing). 18-case TestValidateSchema + TestRunJSON_DeepValidationTriggersRepair (a wrong-enum body that the old shallow check + typed unmarshal accepted now repairs). go test ./... green (18 pkg ok); scripted eval gate precision=1.000 recall=1.000; gofmt+vet clean.

title: funnel: reserve downstream (verify/repro) budget so finders can't starve them
description: SYMPTOM (user 2026-06-17): triage+repro are effectively always ignored because finders blow through the per-cycle token limit. ROOT CAUSE: the per-run TokenBudget is a SINGLE shared agent.BudgetPool charged by all roles. budgetState.overHard/overSoft compare CUMULATIVE TOTAL chargeable spend against the single budget. The finder stage (Stage A, hypothesize) launches first and in bulk, so finders drain the whole pool before any candidate reaches Stage C (verify); verify's gate (verify_stream.go:67 budget.overHard) then sees total>=budget and orphans every candidate as T3. No T2 survivors => repro (which depends on T2 input; repro spend is on a SEPARATE per-day ledger per bugbot-3lt) never has anything to reproduce. bugbot-3lt live evidence: 769 finder units skipped_hard_budget, 0 T1 promotions across 15 runs.
design: FIX = partition the per-run TokenBudget into a finder sub-budget (share*budget) and a reserved verify sub-budget (the remainder), each gated independently by its role so verify keeps full 3-refuter strength WITHIN its reserve instead of degrading the instant finders fill their share. Implementation (dual-mode, opt-in so direct-budgetState unit tests stay legacy): budgetState keeps the existing total 'pool' (legacy single-pool mode, unchanged) and gains finderPool/verifyPool + finderBudget/verifyBudget, populated only by a new reserveForDownstream(share) called from run.go with the resolved Options.FinderBudgetShare (default DefaultFinderBudgetShare=0.7 => reserve 30%). spendRecorder routes each completion's chargeable tokens to finderPool when ev.Role=='finder' else verifyPool (nil-safe), in addition to the total pool. Gates: hypothesize finder gate -> finderOverHard/finderOverSoft (finderPool vs finderBudget); verify_stream verify gate -> verifyOverHard/verifyOverSoft (verifyPool vs verifyBudget); both fall back to legacy overHard/overSoft when their sub-pool is nil. runnerLimits refactored to runnerLimitsForPool(base,pool); finder uses finderRunnerLimits, verify+arbiter use verifyRunnerLimits. share>=1 or unlimited budget => reserveForDownstream is a no-op (legacy single pool). Config: budgets.finder_budget_share (0..1; 0=>funnel default), env BUGBOT_BUDGETS_FINDER_BUDGET_SHARE, validated 0..1, wired through cli/scan.go. Repro budgeting UNCHANGED (separate per-day ledger by design, bugbot-3lt).
acceptance_criteria: 1) New funnel test proves a Sweep that would (legacy) exhaust the pool on finders now VERIFIES at least one candidate to T2 under the default reservation. 2) budgetState unit tests: reserveForDownstream partition math, recorder role routing into finderPool/verifyPool, finderOverHard/verifyOverHard thresholds. 3) Verify-budget-exhaustion still orphans as T3 (reworked TestSweep_BudgetOrphanPersistsAsTier3). 4) Existing finder-degradation tests preserved by FinderBudgetShare=1.0 (legacy mode). 5) config: finder_budget_share parses, env-overrides, validates 0..1; default 0 defers to funnel. 6) go build + go test ./internal/funnel/ ./internal/config/ ./internal/cli/ green.
notes: Config knobs: budgets.finder_token_claim / budgets.verifier_token_claim (env BUGBOT_BUDGETS_FINDER_TOKEN_CLAIM / _VERIFIER_TOKEN_CLAIM), Options.FinderTokenClaim/VerifierTokenClaim, default funnel.DefaultTokenClaim=1_000_000; 0=default, negative=disable per-task cap. Wired through scan/daemon/review CLI. Core change: internal/funnel/funnel.go runnerLimitsForPool(base,pool,claim) + finder/verifyRunnerLimits pass the role claim; run.go sets budget.finderClaim/verifyClaim. Tests (internal/funnel): reworked TestSweep_BudgetOrphanPersistsAsTier3 (2 cands, share 0.5, verify reserve exhausted by cand1's panel -> cand1 survives T2, cand2 orphaned T3 at verifyOverHard gate); TestBudgetReserve_VerifyGateIndependentOfFinderSpend (gate-level prioritization); TestRunnerLimits_ClaimCap; TestSpendRecorder_ClaimRefundIsAutomatic; TestResolve_TokenClaimDefaults; config TestEnvOverride_TokenClaims. go build + go test ./internal/... green (17 pkgs), -race green on budget tests.
close_reason: Implemented the claimant budget system: per-task token claim (default 1M, configurable per role) caps each finder/verifier run; unspent budget is automatically returned to the per-role pool because only actual spend is charged. Downstream prioritization preserved via the existing per-role reserve (FinderBudgetShare). Rejected the explicit reserve/refund ledger on oracle review (same-role starvation + sentinel misclassification, no benefit). All acceptance criteria met; tests green incl. -race.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: Delivered the claimant budget system (user directive 2026-06-17: finders/verifiers claim a per-task budget, default 1M, unspent returned to pool). Final design after oracle review: KEEP the spend-based per-role reservation (finderPool/verifyPool sized by FinderBudgetShare; gates finderOverHard/verifyOverHard; BudgetCheck->TruncBudgetPool orphan) — this is the prioritization that stops finders starving verify. ADD a per-task CLAIM as a per-run cap in budgetState.runnerLimitsForPool: each finder/verifier run's TokenBudget = min(role claim, base, sub-pool remaining). 'Return to pool on closure' is AUTOMATIC: the recorder charges the sub-pool only for tokens ACTUALLY spent, so the unspent part of a claim is never removed and stays available to siblings. REJECTED the explicit reserve-and-refund ledger (reservable counter + acquire/release): oracle confirmed it adds a same-role starvation bug (first verifier reserves the whole verify reserve -> concurrent candidates denied -> FEWER T2 survivors) and a verifier sentinel misclassification, while buying nothing the spend pool doesn't already give. Net: ~per-run cap + config knobs, zero launch-site rewires, no new concurrency state.

title: Umbrella: --repro inconsistent / low-yield — decomposed root causes (live dogfood evidence)
description:
SYMPTOM (user-reported 2026-06-17): `bugbot scan --repro` produces inconsistent and low-yield results; reporter suspected a sandbox issue but also a token/budgeting/pipelining issue. Investigation against the LIVE dogfood state DB (.bugbot/state.db, 15 scan runs) decomposes the symptom into distinct, independently-fixable causes. '--repro inconsistent' is mostly the visible tip of an upstream finder-reliability + budget problem, plus a latent repro-correctness hazard and a sandbox/image capability gap. This is a TRACKING issue: it depends on the child causes below.

LIVE EVIDENCE (cumulative, 15 runs):
- findings: 4 total, ALL tier-2 open, 0 promoted to T1 (repro_path empty, needs_human=0).
- reproducer agent_units: 1 exhausted, 1 infra_error, 0 reproduced — repro effectively never succeeds.
- finder agent_units: 700 parse_failed, 769 skipped_hard_budget, 48 skipped_degraded, 24 budget_stopped, 81 ok. Several individual runs show 125/125 finders parse_failed with 0 ok.
- sandbox image (bugbot.yaml) = debian:stable-slim: NO Go toolchain, so the reproducer cannot build/run the target (Go) repo's tests.

CONTRIBUTING CAUSES (each its own bead):
1. bugbot-vig (P1) — interpret() (internal/repro/interpret.go:50) treats ANY non-zero exit not matching a Go-ONLY marker blacklist as 'demonstrated'. Latent in this DB (nothing reached repro) but the moment a finding does, an env/build failure's non-zero exit is inconsistently promoted to T1. This is the core 'inconsistent' code hazard.
2. bugbot-5au (P2) — no per-ecosystem sandbox capability probe; debian:stable-slim has no `go`, the reproducer emits Go invocations that exit 127 -> environment_error -> exhausted/infra_error, with no upfront 'image cannot run this ecosystem' signal. This is the 'sandbox issue' the reporter sensed.
3. bugbot-1q0 / bugbot-rwe (P1/P2) — mass finder parse_failed (700 cumulative; per-run 125/125) from MiniMax-M3 think/output-token exhaustion mid-JSON. Starves repro of T2 findings (only 4 ever reached verify-survived).
4. bugbot-v3z (P1) — capability profile inert: ContextWindow never scales chunk/read/compaction; combined with whole-repo `**/*` scope + 10M per-cycle budget this yields 769 hard-stop skips. The 'token/budgeting/pipelining' the reporter suspected.
5. bugbot-dj7 (P1, incidental) — a long --repro run aborted with SQLITE_IOERR_SHORT_READ (522), a write-volume/WAL-checkpoint race; '--repro inconsistent' may also surface as the scan crashing mid-run.

PIPELINING WAS CHECKED AND IS NOT A CAUSE: the in-run repro queue (reproQueue buffer=16 + overflow drain, repro_hook.go), the IDLE-slot consumer, the claim check (ReproPath/NeedsHuman re-read before and after slot acquisition), and the post-verify catch-up drain (reproAttempted sync.Map) were traced in run.go:575-641 and repro_hook.go:33-123 — delivery is exactly-once and the worker join (reproWg.Wait after reproQ.ch close) is correct. Repro LLM spend is ledgered via a SEPARATE recorder (cli/scan.go newLedgerRecorder) so it counts toward per_day totals but NOT the funnel's per-run TokenBudget pool — this is documented design, not a defect. Record this so the next agent does not re-investigate the pipeline.
design: Tracking/umbrella only — no code lands here. Add `bd dep add <this> <child>` for each contributing cause so this issue shows blocked-by the real fixes and `bd ready` surfaces the children first. Suggested fix ORDER for a future agent: (1) bugbot-vig — make repro outcomes trustworthy/consistent (cheap, self-contained, highest leverage on the 'inconsistent' symptom); (2) bugbot-1q0/bugbot-rwe + bugbot-v3z — restore finder yield so repro has T2 input; (3) bugbot-5au — capability probe so the reproducer stops emitting impossible invocations; (4) bugbot-dj7 — store write-volume hardening for long runs. Operational note for reproducing the symptom: dogfood bugbot.yaml uses debian:stable-slim (no Go); switch to a golang image (integration tests use golang:1.26-alpine) before expecting any Go finding to reach genuine T1.
acceptance_criteria: Each child cause (bugbot-vig, bugbot-5au, bugbot-1q0/bugbot-rwe, bugbot-v3z, bugbot-dj7) is either fixed or carries an explicit determination. End state: a `--repro` run on a toolchain-capable image yields DETERMINISTIC T1 promotions for genuinely reproducible findings and an honest 'could not reproduce (environment)' otherwise — never a non-deterministic T1 from a bare non-zero exit. Finder parse-failure rate on the bugbot self-scan corpus is measured and bounded so repro receives a non-trivial T2 stream.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text:
Landed PR #78 (squash ae76627) addressing sandbox-side root causes from this umbrella, dogfooded live on golang:1.26-alpine:
- Image: dogfood bugbot.yaml moved off debian:stable-slim (no Go) to golang:1.26-alpine; toolchain-image requirement now warned in the starter template and surfaced by the new `bugbot prime` command (partial mitigation for bugbot-5au / bugbot-tf1; the runtime capability PROBE itself is still open).
- New root cause #1 (infra): cold Go build cache (GOCACHE/$WORK) overran the 512MiB /tmp tmpfs -> 'no space left on device' -> environment_error. Fixed by relocating GOCACHE/GOTMPDIR onto the disk-backed workspace for every dep strategy (resolveGo).
- New root cause #2 (infra): fixed wall-clock sandbox timeout killed slow-but-progressing cold builds. Replaced with a dynamic idle watchdog (output + workspace-fs + container-CPU fallback); absolute timeout kept as ceiling; new sandbox.idle_timeout_seconds (default 120).
- Live result: the one finding with a terminating generated test ('FunctionResponse Name set to call ID') now promotes to T1 deterministically. The string-truncation findings still 'timeout' because the reproducer emits NON-TERMINATING tests -> filed as bugbot-opq (new child cause; reproducer-agent quality, not sandbox).

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text:
NEW C++ DOGFOOD EVIDENCE (user 2026-06-19, `bugbot repro` on ~/code/personal/services-runtime, an SDL/C++ project, runtime=podman): 10 eligible, 3 attempted, 0 promoted to T1. New contributing causes filed, both P2:
- bugbot-gav: C/C++ memory-safety findings are structurally unreprovable (leaks exit_zero, uninitialized reads not_demonstrated). No sanitizer path in repro: (a) no sanitizer guidance in cmake/meson/generic C/C++ prompt; (b) interpret()/ecosystem.go has no sanitizer/valgrind ran-markers AND the cpp 'error: ' build-marker swallows AddressSanitizer's ERROR: line ahead of the ran-evidence check; (c) detectEcosystem maps only `ctest` to cpp, so the direct-binary cmd the cmake guidance itself suggests lands in 'unknown'. This extends the umbrella beyond its Go-centric children.
- bugbot-xli: agent RunJSON hard-errors on trailing content after a complete JSON value ('invalid character , after top-level value') — distinct from the bugbot-rwe/1q0 truncation class; aborted the third finding before any sandbox run.

title: Blackboard leads: consumed leads are never deleted (zombie rows persist on the blackboard)
description: ConsumeLeads (internal/store/leads.go) only flips status='posted'->'consumed' and stamps consumed_at; the row is never deleted. Migration 004 + cli/leads.go treat consumed rows as permanent 'history'. Live dogfood state.db (.bugbot/state.db) shows 3 api-contract-misuse leads, ALL status=consumed, 0 pending — they persist indefinitely. The deterministic miner re-posts the identical (target_lens,file,line) every scan; AddLead's ON CONFLICT flips consumed->posted, the funnel re-consumes, so the same rows oscillate forever and never leave the blackboard. User expectation: a lead should be consumed AND deleted once its target lens has triaged it.
design:
FIX DIRECTION (confirmed with user 2026-06-17): DELETE-ON-CONSUME. ConsumeLeads must DELETE the claimed rows, not flip status='consumed'. The funnel already copies pending leads into the in-memory leadsByLens map BEFORE the finder runs (internal/funnel/hypothesize.go ~185-216), so deleting the DB row does not affect lead injection into finder tasks. Failure semantics are identical to today's consume-at-claim trade-off: a finder that dies after its lead is claimed loses the claim; the deterministic miner/analyzer re-post next cycle via a fresh AddLead INSERT (the old row is gone).

CLEAN CUTOVER (no shims, migrate every caller):
- internal/store/leads.go: ConsumeLeads -> `DELETE FROM leads WHERE id IN (...)` in the existing single transaction. The consumed->posted flip branch of AddLead's ON CONFLICT (and the consumed_at=NULL reset) becomes dead for the consume path; simplify so ON CONFLICT only refreshes note/poster/scan_run_id for a STILL-PENDING re-post.
- Lead.Status / Lead.ConsumedAt and scanLead()'s consumed_at handling lose their only writer. Recommend removing the consumed-row CONCEPT: drop Status/ConsumedAt from the struct + scanLead + ListLeads selects. Schema: leave the status/consumed_at columns in place (modernc.org/sqlite has limited ALTER DROP COLUMN; a no-op column is harmless) OR add 008_*.sql if a clean drop is wanted.
- internal/cli/leads.go: renderLeads currently renders consumed rows as 'history' and prints 'N lead(s), M pending' + consumedNote()/age(); collapse to pending-only output, update the command long-help ('consumed rows are the history') and the package doc comment.
- internal/cli/worldstate.go: already uses ListLeads(pendingOnly=true) -> unaffected.

TESTS TO MIGRATE:
- internal/store/leads_test.go TestLeads_ConsumeLeads: assert the rows are DELETED (PendingLeads + ListLeads both empty) instead of status='consumed'.
- internal/cli/leads_test.go: drop consumed-history rendering assertions; assert pending-only output.
- internal/funnel/leads_test.go: PendingLeads==0 after Sweep and Stats.LeadsConsumed still hold (no change needed).
- internal/analyzer + internal/miner tests use PendingLeads only -> unaffected.
acceptance_criteria: A lead consumed by its target lens during a run is removed from the blackboard after that run's triage (not left as a status='consumed' zombie). bugbot leads / bugbot status no longer surface already-triaged leads. Pending leads (posted, not yet claimed by an active lens) are unaffected. Tests cover: consumed lead absent post-run; pending lead for an inactive lens survives; re-post after deletion re-raises cleanly.
notes: Evidence pulled from live state.db 2026-06-17: leads table = 3 rows all consumed (api-contract-misuse, poster miner:doc-contradiction). Consume happens at CLAIM time in funnel/hypothesize.go (pre-finder), copied into in-memory leadsByLens, so deleting the DB row does not affect injection into the finder task. Failure semantics of delete-on-consume == current consume-at-claim (a failed finder loses the claim; miner re-posts next cycle). cli/leads.go renderLeads + worldstate.go + store/leads_test.go + cli/leads_test.go reference consumed-row history and must be migrated on a clean cutover.
close_reason: Delete-on-consume implemented: ConsumeLeads now DELETEs claimed rows in its single tx (was status='consumed' flip); AddLead ON CONFLICT simplified to refresh note/poster/scan_run_id only; Lead.Status/ConsumedAt concept removed (columns left inert in schema, no migration); cli renderLeads collapsed to pending-only. Tests assert deletion (gone from PendingLeads+ListLeads), inactive-lens survival, and clean re-post. Merged to main (--no-ff).

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: RELATED FOLLOW-UP (not in scope here, do NOT lose it): delete-on-consume removes the zombie ROWS but does NOT stop the deterministic doc-contradiction miner (internal/miner) and analyzer seed from RE-RAISING the identical (target_lens,file,line) lead every single scan — the finder will keep getting the same lead injected and re-investigating it each cycle (wasted finder budget). User explicitly chose delete-on-consume over 'stop the miner re-raising' for THIS pass, accepting the re-raise churn for now. If that churn becomes a budget problem, file a follow-up to make the seeders skip a suspicion that was already claimed for unchanged code (e.g. key on content hash).

title: eval: live-model scorecard (weak-vs-strong precision/recall/cost) behind keys
description: Eval measures pipeline discrimination but cannot A/B a real weak vs strong model on the same corpus. Add a key-gated live mode that runs the real funnel per configured model over the corpus and emits a per-model scorecard: recall, FP-rate, cost-per-true-bug, tokens. Grow corpus toward real historical bugs.
acceptance_criteria: bugbot eval --live (key-gated) runs >=1 model over BuiltinCases and prints a per-model scorecard with precision/recall/cost-per-true-bug; offline gate unaffected.

title: llm/funnel: wire WithSerializedToolCalls + capability-driven chunk/read/compaction scaling
description: Capabilities is inert in production: WithSerializedToolCalls is never called (only tests), and ContextWindow never scales chunk size/read caps/compaction threshold. One-size-fits-all pipeline silently overflows small-context weak models. Wire serialization in registry.NewClient; scale DefaultChunkSize/finder read caps/history compaction from ContextWindow.
acceptance_criteria: Non-parallel-capable clients are serialized in production; finder chunk size and read caps derive from ContextWindow when known; unit tests cover the scaling and serialization wrapping.
close_reason: WithSerializedToolCalls now wired as outermost decorator in registry.NewClient (no-op for parallel-capable providers; serializes openai-compatible in production). New funnel.scaleFinderForContext derives finder chunk size + read caps from finder ContextWindow when >0, strict no-op when unknown(0)/large(>=64k) so existing behavior is stable; explicit Options values preserved. Unit tests cover serialization wrapping + scaling. Merged to main.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: LIVE EVIDENCE (.bugbot/state.db, 2026-06-17): inert capability scaling manifests as 769 skipped_hard_budget finder units across 15 runs — whole-repo '**/*' scope + fixed chunk/read caps + 10M per-cycle budget on MiniMax-M3 (no caching) exhausts the pool and sheds most units. The 'token/budgeting' half of the user's '--repro inconsistent' report. Umbrella: bugbot-3lt.

title: llm: native structured-output (Request.ResponseSchema) across all adapters + config opt-in
description: Add ResponseSchema (and ResponseSchemaName) to llm.Request. OpenAI/openai-compatible: set response_format json_schema (honor even with tools). Google: responseJsonSchema + application/json, only when no tools (Gemini cannot combine with function-calling). Anthropic: synthetic forced output tool (tool_choice=tool, input_schema=schema) only when no tools, surface tool input as Response.Text. Gate via Capabilities.StructuredOutput; add config Provider.structured_output override so openai-compatible (MiniMax-M3) can opt in.
acceptance_criteria: Each adapter has a unit test (fake server) asserting the request carries the schema in the right field and that tools-present requests behave safely; openai-compatible caps overridable from config; go vet + go test ./internal/llm/... green.
close_reason: Added llm.Request.ResponseSchema + ResponseSchemaName. OpenAI/openai-compatible: response_format json_schema (honored even with tools). Google: responseJsonSchema + application/json (only when no tools). Anthropic: synthetic forced-output tool + tool_choice, forced tool input surfaced as Response.Text (only when no tools). All gated on Capabilities.StructuredOutput. Added Provider.structured_output *bool config override (registry applies it, e.g. MiniMax openai-compatible opt-in). 14 fake-server unit tests across all adapters incl. tools-present-safe + cap-gating + config override. go vet + go test ./internal/llm/... green. Merged.

title: agent: thread answer schema through runner/RunJSON + shallow shape validation + constrained repair
description: Thread RunJSON schema into llm.Request.ResponseSchema (capability-gated). Apply on completions; adapters guard tool-presence. Add shallow root-level schema validation (type + required props, lenient on items/additionalProperties) to RunJSON accept gate so wrong-shape-but-valid-JSON (bare array, missing refuted) is caught. On parse/validate failure, repair via an immediate tools-less natively-constrained finalization completion (run with maxIterations=0).
acceptance_criteria: RunJSON sends ResponseSchema only when StructuredOutput cap is set; validation routes missing-required to repair; repair completion is tools-less + schema-bearing; existing runjson_test cases stay green; new tests cover validation-triggered repair and the no-cap passthrough path.
close_reason: RunJSON now threads its schema natively into llm.Request.ResponseSchema through run/completeOnce/complete, gated at the agent layer on Capabilities().StructuredOutput (no-cap path sends no schema = legacy behavior). Added shallow root-shape validation (root type + required keys, lenient) to the accept gate so wrong-shape-but-valid-JSON routes to repair. Repair is now a single tools-less, schema-bearing completion (final=true drops tools) so capable adapters apply native grammar-constrained decoding. A budget-truncated run SKIPS the repair and returns its budget outcome, preserving bugbot-1q0's budget-stop classification + overshoot bound. Merged: overseer salvaged the cancelled fixer's complete+passing-but-uncommitted implementation and added the budget-skip guard that fixed a funnel TestRunFinder_BudgetStopNotParseFailure regression the agent's agent-only test scope missed.

title: Schema-constrained (grammar) output at every agent/phase boundary
description: North-star lever for weak models: force every agent->phase JSON boundary (finder candidates, refuter/arbiter refutation, reproducer plan, patch plan) to fit its declared JSON schema via provider-native structured output (grammar-constrained decoding) plus a harness-side shape guarantee.
